phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q the wave contacts — the windblast's tornado tick chunks mages who stand in it for the full duration.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W charge threat begins: anyone who steps to punish your Q eats a taunt audition.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Slight edge at level 3: your anti-magic shield trades beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 the numbers lean your way — anti-magic shield trades edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight deficit at the level-6 breakpoint: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your anti-magic shield trades on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your anti-magic shield trades in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Anivia's wall-frost poke control own this window — your anti-magic shield trades don't answer them at two-plus items yet. Farm what's safe and let the next spike re-price the lane.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 your anti-magic shield trades beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W charge threat begins: anyone who steps to punish your Q eats a taunt audition.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: E-dash into W-taunt is the lane's rudest counter-engage. Magic damage starts paying your shield.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Your anti-magic shield trades out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control through levels 4-5 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint your anti-magic shield trades beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: First item spike: the full taunt rotation chunks and your MR makes AP trades a subscription you sell.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Galio is the anti-engage: taunt their dive, shield their magic, and R the teammate they thought was alone.

phases.why: First item spike: the full taunt rotation chunks and your MR makes AP trades a subscription you sell.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Galio is the anti-engage: taunt their dive, shield their magic, and R the teammate they thought was alone.
